events.location: Committee Room, House Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs, Washington, D.C.
events.description: Subcommittee on Indian Affairs hearing on H.R. 11036 and S. 3698 to amend Act of June 4, 1920 regarding Crow Tribe land allotments, chaired by James A. Haley
Act of Congress approved ratifying treaty of May 7, 1868, creating Crow Indian Reservation (15 Stat. 649)
Treaty between US government and Crow Tribe creating Crow Indian Reservation; II Kappler 1008
Treaty between Crow Tribe and US government ceding second tract of land between Boulder and Clarks Fork Rivers; I Kappler 195
Act ratifying Treaty of June 12, 1880, authorizing survey and allotment of Crow Reservation lands (22 Stat. 42)
Act of May 8, 1906 (34 Stat. 182) enacted, authorizing Secretary of Interior to issue fee simple patents and remove restrictions on Indian allotments
Act of June 4, 1920 (41 Stat. 751) enacted, providing for allotment of Crow Tribe lands including Section 2 acreage limitations on conveyances
Hearing before Senate Committee on Indian Affairs, Irrigation and Reclamation of Arid Lands on Crow allotment bill; proceedings in Volume 149; Crow Delegation including Robert Yellowtail attended
Crow Delegation of seven members sent to Washington D.C. to urge passage of bill to authorize survey and allotment of Crow Reservation lands; Robert Yellowtail only surviving member
Act of June 8, 1940 (54 Stat. 252) enacted, amending 1920 Crow Act to permit Secretary of Interior to approve sales to Crow members without acreage limitations under certain circumstances
Commissioner of Indian Affairs suspended all sales of land on Crow Reservation upon discovery of still-existing acreage limitations and ordered inquiry into extent of violations
Crow Tribal Council passed two resolutions (Resolution No. 77 and incorporated resolution) requesting Congress to repeal Section 2 of 1920 Act and validate prior conveyances; passed by majority vote
H.R. 11036 introduced by Lee Metcalf in House of Representatives, referred to Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
S. 3698 referred to House Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
S. 3698 passed the Senate; attested by Felton M. Johnston, Secretary
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs submitted report on S. 3698, reporting favorably with amendments; ordered to be printed June 5 (legislative day June 4), 1956
Act of July 27, 1868 (15 Stat. 228) transferred control of Indian affairs from Secretary of Treasury to Department of Interior

financial_transactions.description: By various treaties, Indian tribes relinquished and ceded lands to United States for nominal consideration, retaining reservations

financial_transactions.payee: individual Crow Indians with fee patents
financial_transactions.description: Indian lands purchased by white owners from Indians who had fee patents, some in violation of 1920 Act acreage limitations

financial_transactions.payer: single purchasers (some buying many thousand acres)
financial_transactions.description: Dead allotments advertised and sold at public government-supervised sale; single purchasers sometimes bought many thousand acres; fee patents issued directly to purchaser
Penalty under Section 2 of 1920 Act for accepting conveyance in violation of acreage limitations; also imprisonment not more than six months or both

financial_transactions.date: hypothetical future scenario described in memorandum
financial_transactions.description: Feared scenario where speculators would buy tract less than 640 acres from former Indian owner for small consideration and force adjacent landowner to buy out at outrageous price

correspondence.sender_title: Attorney for Crow Reservation Landowners Association
correspondence.subject: Memorandum on S. 3698 - Bill to Repeal Paragraph 1 of Section 2 of Act of June 4, 1920 and Validate Certain Conveyances, Crow Indian Reservation, Montana
correspondence.action_requested: Passage of S. 3698 to repeal acreage limitations and validate prior conveyances
correspondence.outcome: Statement made part of the record by unanimous consent

correspondence.subject: Resolution incorporated with Resolution No. 77 - Land Sales on Crow Reservation
correspondence.action_requested: Secretary to submit bill to Congress to repeal Section 2 of 1920 Act and clear titles; resume sales of restricted and trust lands upon passage
correspondence.outcome: Passed by majority vote of Crow Tribal Council

correspondence.subject: Resolution No. 77 - Repealing certain provisions of Section 2 of Act of June 4, 1920
correspondence.action_requested: Enact law repealing first paragraph of Section 2 of 1920 Act and validating prior conveyances; authorized Tribal Attorney and Tribal Delegates to introduce bills and appear before committees
correspondence.outcome: Passed and adopted by majority vote; approved by William Wall, Chairman

legislative_actions.committee: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Passed Senate June 11, 1956; attested by Felton M. Johnston, Secretary

legislative_actions.committee: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Reported favorably with amendments; amendments added language on legal rights for access and ways of necessity, and mineral rights reservation to allottee

legislative_actions.committee: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Amended to add on line 10 after 'law': comma and phrase 'including legal rights for access and ways of necessity'; added proviso at end of line 10 regarding mineral rights

legislative_actions.committee: House Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Referred to Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
Referred to House Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs on June 1, 1956
Passed Senate as S. 3779; described as non-controversial, cure of technicality in bill passed two years prior
Enacted; provided for allotment of Crow Tribe lands; Section 2 imposed acreage limitations on conveyances
Enacted; amended 1920 Crow Act to permit Secretary of Interior to approve sales to Crow members without acreage limitations under certain circumstances
Enacted; ratified Treaty of June 12, 1880; authorized survey and allotment of Crow Reservation lands; Section 2 authorized Secretary of Interior to cause surveys and issue patents
Enacted; authorized Secretary of Interior to issue fee simple patents and remove all restrictions on sale, encumbrance, or taxation of Indian allotments
